title: Pyrus majuscula

Based on the related Pyrus species, here is a short paragraph about the plant Pyrus communis:

Pyrus communis, commonly known as the common pear, is a deciduous tree species within the rose family (Rosaceae). The fruit of Pyrus communis has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the fruit of Pyrus communis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and jams, which are believed to have various health benefits and are commonly used to support digestive health.

The species is further classified as a member of the subgenus Pyrus, which contains species of pear trees that are native to Asia. Pyrus majuscula is also known by its synonym Pyrus bactrianica.
